title: rudbeckia hirta var. serotina
intro: Rudbeckia hirta var. serotina, also known as Black-eyed Susan, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow petals and dark centers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rudbeckia hirta var. serotina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
